classObjective: Graph the solution set of a compound inequality on a number line and write compound inequalities that model real-world situations and interpret the solutions in context.
workedExamples: 6-3, Example 3 — Write Compound Inequalities from Graphs
warmUp: Graph the compound inequality: x > 2 or x < -1
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: bounded and unbounded intervals; temperature range and BMI contexts; writing inequalities from visual graphs
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice graphing compound inequalities and writing them from real-world contexts
independentPractice: Practice problems graphing compound inequality solutions and writing inequalities for real-world situations
assessment: Exit ticket: write and graph a compound inequality for acceptable room temperature (between 65°F and 78°F inclusive)
capReflection: How do real-world constraints like temperature ranges naturally form compound inequalities?
